news.t: These bond funds shield you from interest-rate shocks — but here's the catch
These ETFs can be your insurance against Fed hikes and rising rates
How to immunize your bond ETF if interest rates turn back up
To hedge or not to hedge?
How you can avoid bond fund losses when rates rise

overview.description: The iShares Interest Rate Hedged Corporate Bond ETF (LQDH) is an exchange-traded fund that mostly invests in investment grade fixed income. The fund tracks an index that holds iShares iBoxx USD Investment Grade Corporate Bond ETF (LQD), which targets USD-denominated investment corporate bonds, while mitigating duration risk. LQDH was launched on May 27, 2014 and is issued by BlackRock.
infoTable: BlackRock Interest Rate Hedged Corporate Bond Index

data.title: These bond funds shield you from interest-rate shocks — but here's the catch
data.text: Insurance against Fed rate hikes with “rate-hedged” ETFs doesn't come cheap.

data.title: These ETFs can be your insurance against Fed hikes and rising rates
data.text: “Rate-hedged” bond funds can make money when interest rates rise and bond prices fall.

data.title: How to immunize your bond ETF if interest rates turn back up
data.text: The best time to buy insurance is when whatever it is you're insuring against is considered unlikely. So, should you hedge your bond portfolio against rising interest rates?

data.title: To hedge or not to hedge?
data.text: Interest rate hedges cut both ways.

data.title: How you can avoid bond fund losses when rates rise
data.text: Bond funds that don't lose money when interest rates rise? Sounds too good to be true, doesn't it?
